title: Locator Strategies
description: Master Playwright's powerful locator strategies to reliably find and interact with elements on web pages.
objectives: Understand what locators are and why they're important
Learn different locator strategies (text, CSS, role, test-id)
Choose the right locator for different scenarios
Use locator chaining and filtering techniques

sections.title: Understanding Locators
sections.content:
# What are Locators?

Locators are Playwright's way of finding elements on a page. Unlike traditional methods that return elements immediately, Playwright locators are:

**Lazy**: They don't find elements until an action is performed
**Auto-waiting**: They automatically wait for elements to be ready
**Resilient**: They retry if elements are not found initially
**Strict**: They throw errors if multiple matches are found

## Why Locators Matter

Your tests are only as reliable as your locators. Good locators:
- Survive application changes
- Are readable and maintainable
- Don't break when page structure changes
- Reflect user perspective

## The Locator API

```typescript
const locator = page.locator('selector');
await locator.click(); // Action triggers element search
```

Key point: The locator doesn't search for the element when created, only when you perform an action!

sections.title: Text-Based Locators
sections.explanation: Text locators find elements by their visible text. They're the most resilient because they match what users actually see.

sections.title: Role-Based Locators
sections.explanation: Role-based locators use ARIA roles to find elements. They're excellent for accessible applications and reflect semantic meaning.

sections.title: CSS and XPath Selectors
sections.explanation: CSS and XPath selectors provide powerful ways to locate elements by structure and attributes. Use them when semantic locators aren't available.

sections.title: Test ID Locators
sections.explanation: Test IDs provide the most stable locators but require adding special attributes to your HTML. Best for elements that change frequently.

sections.title: Locator Chaining and Filtering
sections.explanation: Chaining and filtering allow you to narrow down elements precisely. This creates more specific and reliable locators.

sections.title: Choosing the Right Locator
Common Pitfalls

keyTakeaways: Locators are lazy and auto-wait, making tests more reliable
Prefer user-facing locators: getByRole(), getByText(), getByLabel()
Use getByTestId() for stability when user-facing attributes aren't available
Chain and filter locators to make them more specific
Playwright is strict - it throws errors if multiple elements match
Avoid fragile selectors based on styling classes or deep DOM structure
Test IDs provide the most stable locators for dynamic content
Good locators survive application changes and reflect user perspective
